items.title: openSmartItem opens unvalidated feed-controlled URL with chrome.tabs.create (no scheme hardening)
items.action: Before chrome.tabs.create({ url: item.url }) in smart-folders.ts:189, parse new URL(item.url) and require protocol http:/https:, warn-dropping otherwise, mirroring openUrl/openSmartFolderListing.
items.rationale: Only open-path that skips scheme validation while ingesting attacker-controlled RSS link hrefs, allowing file:/data:/chrome: schemes — highest real-world exploitability despite medium label.

items.title: optional_host_permissions grants all-hosts (https://*/* and http://*/*)
items.action: Replace standing https://*/* and http://*/* in manifest.json with gesture-bound per-origin grants derived from baseUrl via originPatternForBaseUrl, or document the threat model if a wildcard is unavoidable.
items.rationale: A single user-approved all-hosts grant exposes ambient GitLab/Jira cookies to every site, a broad SSRF/exfiltration surface and CWS-review liability that undercuts the codebase's least-privilege posture.

items.title: Options pages read chrome.storage.local and cast directly to AppState without Zod parsing
items.action: Replace the bare `as { state: AppState }` casts in BackupRestore.svelte and FeedSubscriptions.svelte with readPersistedState() from shared/chrome/storage.ts, which migrates and validates.
items.rationale: Trust-boundary violation on external chrome.storage input across a whole surface, where a corrupt or unmigrated envelope silently passes as valid AppState and crashes downstream consumers.

items.title: onStateBroadcast casts raw chrome.runtime message to StateBroadcastMessage without validating .state field
items.action: After m.type === 'lunma/state-broadcast' in messages.ts:249, validate m.state via AppStateV7Schema.safeParse() (or at minimum assert an object with numeric schemaVersion) before widening to StateBroadcastMessage.
items.rationale: Unvalidated cross-surface state seeds the sidebar store via Object.assign, so a stale/old-SW snapshot propagates undefined required keys into reactive state — a high-severity correctness boundary affecting every sidebar boot.

items.title: BackupRestore.svelte passes unvalidated JSON.parse output to bus.send as BackupEnvelope
items.action: Parse pendingImportData through BackupEnvelopeSchema.safeParse() in confirmImport() before bus.send, storing the validated BackupEnvelope and showing validation errors inline.
items.rationale: User-file content is cast from unknown to a typed bus payload with the only runtime guard being the downstream bus adapter — a high-severity local safety gap, though the adapter currently catches it.

items.title: All 4 connector modules eagerly imported in hot SW boot path
items.action: Convert the top-level connector imports in smart-folders.ts:6-9 to a lazy Map<source, () => Promise<SourceConnector>> registry loaded on first fetchSmartFolderRuntime() of that source.
items.rationale: ~52KB/~11KB-gzip parsed on every SW boot for users who may use no connectors — the only high-severity startup cost and the clearest perf win.

items.title: openUrl handler non-http(s) scheme blocking not unit-tested
items.action: Add tests in coordinator.handlers.test.ts asserting openUrl drops javascript:, ftp:, and unparseable URLs (no tabs.create, ok ack) using installSavedTabChromeStub()+makeCoordinator().
items.rationale: The scheme guard is the authoritative defense for the boundary-divert and launcher open paths, yet a regression that re-enables bookmarklet injection would pass CI undetected — security-adjacent coverage gap.

items.title: requestLauncherSuggestions returns msg.results as LauncherResult[] with no element-level validation
items.action: Add a LauncherResultSchema to shared/launcher-contract.ts and validate msg.results (and openUrls/ungrantedSources) with .array().safeParse() in messages.ts, filtering non-conforming elements.
items.rationale: Array.isArray-only guards let a divergent SW return malformed result objects, causing silent launcher render bugs at a runtime trust boundary.

items.title: store.state double-unknown cast to SidebarLocalState spreads across three files
items.action: Introduce a SidebarLunmaStore subclass typing state as the existing SidebarState (AppState & SidebarLocalState) intersection so the double-unknown casts in store.svelte.ts, PinnedTabs.svelte, and main.ts disappear.
items.rationale: Double-unknown fully suppresses type safety across three files, so renamed/retyped local fields fail silently — a recurring foot-gun affecting core sidebar state.

items.title: toPendingEvent casts Zod-inferred type to SidebarCommand then SidebarPendingEvent
items.action: Replace the `as SidebarPendingEvent` cast in bus-adapter.ts toPendingEvent with a per-kind discriminated dispatch (or a compile-time satisfies test), and comment the harmless line-80 optional-vs-exactOptional cast.
items.rationale: Broad cast on the command bus could mask a wrong payload shape into a handler if variants grow, but current variants are trivially-shaped so risk is latent.

items.title: saxes tag.attributes cast to Record<string,string> unsafe if namespace mode enabled
items.action: Pass { xmlns: false } to both new SaxesParser() calls in opml.ts and rss.ts so tag.attributes infers Record<string,string> and the cast can be removed.
items.rationale: Cast is correct today but a future xmlns:true reconfiguration would silently yield wrong attribute types feeding feed/OPML parsing — cheap to make a compile error.

items.title: Space rename/recolour revert on chrome.tabGroups.update failure not tested end-to-end
items.location: apps/extension/src/background/handlers/spaces.ts (lines 40–59, 61–80) — apps/extension/src/background/coordinator.tab-groups.test.ts covers renameSpace but not the revert path
items.action: In coordinator.tab-groups.test.ts, stub chrome.tabGroups.update to reject for renameSpace and recolourSpace and assert the store value reverts and the ack carries the error.
items.rationale: Uncovered failure-recovery path means a broken revert would leave state diverged from Chrome silently — a meaningful correctness gap on a user-facing action.

items.title: AppStateV7Schema smartItemBindings slot shape not validated in schemas.test.ts
items.location: apps/extension/src/shared/schemas.test.ts — AppStateV7Schema not imported or exercised; apps/extension/src/shared/schemas.ts lines 183–241
items.action: Add a schemas.test.ts describe block importing AppStateV7Schema directly: valid { tabId, allowGlob } parses, bare-number slot rejects, missing allowGlob rejects.
items.rationale: The current schema version's slot shape has no direct test, so a malformed binding bypassing migration would go unvalidated — gap on the persistence trust boundary.

items.title: GroupOrchestrator.orchestrateActivation preserveFavoriteFocus branch not unit-tested
items.action: Add direct GroupOrchestrator unit tests (mock store + chrome stub) for activeTabIsGlobalFavorite throw→false and preserveFavoriteFocus skip/proceed branches.
items.rationale: Branch-level focus logic is only covered by integration tests, making the query-failure and skip paths hard to isolate on regression.

items.title: queries.ts isTrackedTab, savedTabIdForBoundTab, findSpaceIdByGroupId have no direct unit tests
items.action: Extend queries.test.ts with describe blocks for isTrackedTab, savedTabIdForBoundTab, and findSpaceIdByGroupId covering found/not-found/guard cases.
items.rationale: Three pure predicates gating tab adoption, boundary re-arm, and group lifecycle are only tested indirectly, slowing regression isolation.

items.title: No e2e test for workspace creation flow
items.location: apps/extension/e2e/ — no spec exercises the full createSpace UI flow
items.action: Add apps/extension/e2e/workspace-creation.spec.ts dispatching createSpace, asserting the Space chip renders with correct name/data-space-id and persists across reload, patterned on cross-space-switch.spec.ts.
items.rationale: The core createSpace→broadcast→reconcile→chip render path is only a precondition in other specs, leaving a primary user flow without end-to-end coverage.

items.title: No e2e test for launcher search result navigation
items.action: Extend launcher-overlay.spec.ts (or add launcher-search.spec.ts) to dispatch a query, assert a non-empty result list, and verify selecting a result focuses the existing tab rather than duplicating it.
items.rationale: Real-Chrome history/bookmarks integration and dedup-on-select are never exercised end-to-end, leaving the launcher's primary purpose untested in the real environment.

items.title: Sidebar mounts all UI components eagerly without code splitting
items.action: Lazy-load conditional feature components (SmartFolderEditor, SpaceEditor) in App.svelte via Svelte dynamic import, keeping primitives eager, and measure bundle impact.
items.rationale: 18 static imports inflate initial sidebar parse/eval even for panels never opened — the most impactful surface for code splitting, but needs measurement.

items.title: Synchronous settings read during sidebar boot blocks initial render
items.action: Parallelize the await readSettings() in main.ts:46 with the state snapshot via Promise.all and apply tint/boundary post-mount, keeping only density pre-mount if layout-shift-critical.
items.rationale: Serial storage.sync read gates first paint on cold boot; parallelizing is a clear latency win on the most-used surface.

items.title: No SW resource batching for frequent Chrome API operations
items.action: Collapse the two boot-path tab queries into one chrome.tabs.query({}) and have writeSetting (settings.ts:368-379) merge an in-memory settings cache instead of re-reading all settings on each single-field write.
items.rationale: Redundant IPC/scheduler overhead on boot and every settings write; batching cuts repeated round-trips with modest effort.

items.title: extension-store-publish tasks.md §1.1: wrong release-please output key
items.action: Update tasks.md §1.1, design.md D2, and proposal.md line 21 to use steps.release.outputs['apps/extension--release_created'] matching the manifest-mode workflow.
items.rationale: A binding artifact name (per CLAUDE.md normative-names policy) disagrees with shipped code across three files — concrete spec/code drift on the release path.

items.title: open-source-public-launch tasks.md §4.1: identity required check omitted
items.action: Add identity to the §4.1 required-check list (verify + e2e + dco + identity) and update design.md D6 and the open-source-governance spec to list identity explicitly.
items.rationale: Applying branch protection per the current §4.1 would leave the existing identity guard unenforced at the merge boundary — a real governance gap if executed as written.

items.title: github-repo-and-ci spec delta describes single pnpm -r verify job but fan-out shipped
items.action: Rewrite the spec delta's requirement and Pull-request scenario in specs/release-engineering/spec.md to describe the parallel matrix + site + no-op aggregator structure instead of pnpm -r verify.
items.rationale: On archive this delta becomes the living spec, enshrining an implementation detail that never shipped — must be fixed before archiving.

items.title: open-source-public-launch tasks.md §1.7: unchecked task is implemented on main
items.action: Mark §1.7 [x] in tasks.md with a note referencing commit 3eddb29 (DCO governance landed).
items.rationale: Checkbox drift on a completed task, smaller blast radius than the spec/required-check drifts above but still a docs/code lockstep violation.

items.title: Menu item height hard-coded to 34px instead of --row-h token
items.action: Replace height: 34px with height: var(--row-h) in RowMenu.svelte, Menu.svelte, and ContextMenu.svelte so menu rows become density-aware.
items.rationale: Highest-impact visual finding: menu rows are frozen at Normal density and ignore Compact/Comfort, a functional accessibility/density regression in shared ui primitives.

items.title: Hard-coded z-index values bypassing --z-* token scale
items.action: Replace z-index: 9999 with var(--z-overlay) in DragClone.svelte and all z-index: 2 drop-lines with var(--z-raised); resolve SpaceEditor's 60 against a token (or add --z-popover).
items.rationale: Raw z-indices across six files risk stacking-context bugs and bypass the token scale that governs surface layering.

items.title: FolderRow hard-codes oklch lightness (0.62) and chroma (0.16) for --folder-c
items.action: Compose --folder-c in FolderRow.svelte:207 from colourToOklch(color)'s canonical {l,c,h} (or the --space-* tokens) instead of literal 0.62/0.16.
items.rationale: Hard-coded l/c render folder tints at the wrong identity for red/yellow/gray, a visible colour-fidelity defect against the canonical palette.

items.title: Hard-coded 3px border-radius on favicon images (missing --r-xs token)
items.action: Add a --r-2xs: 3px token (or adopt --r-xs) and replace every border-radius: 3px favicon site with the token variable.
items.rationale: Sub-token literal across six sites won't track radius-scale changes — broad but low-risk token-contract drift.

items.title: overlay.css re-declares base tokens with hard-coded oklch values instead of inheriting
items.action: Add a parity test (like space-palette-parity.test.ts) failing on drift for tokens that must match tokens.css, and annotate intentionally-different values (scrim/glass opacity) in overlay.css.
items.rationale: Shadow-root token copies can silently drift from tokens.css with no guard; a parity test bounds the divergence on a colour-critical surface.

items.title: overlay.css uses hard-coded font shorthand instead of type-scale tokens
items.action: Redeclare the type-scale and weight tokens in overlay.css's shadow-root block and replace each `font:` shorthand with font-size: var(--text-*) + font-weight: var(--weight-*).
items.rationale: Many raw px/weight literals across the overlay won't track the type scale, the largest single concentration of type-token drift.

items.title: New-tab launcher makes synchronous readSettings before mount
items.action: Move the readSettings() await in newtab/main.ts:45 into a Promise.all with the state snapshot so reads run in parallel and mount can use defaults.
items.rationale: Same boot-blocking pattern as the sidebar but on a transient surface, so lower impact.

items.title: chrome.storage.sync reads on boot may block SW if storage unavailable
items.action: Add a ~500ms timeout to readSettings() (settings.ts:341-350) that falls back to DEFAULTS and retries asynchronously post-mount.
items.rationale: Transient sync-storage stalls could delay paint 1-2s with no current guard, but the unavailable-storage case is rare.

items.title: No lazy loading for options surface components
items.action: Defer non-critical options sections (Backup & restore, Feed subscriptions, Recently archived, Advanced) behind scroll/expand, keeping the primary Settings section eager.
items.rationale: Options opens infrequently so parse-cost bloat is the least-visible perf concern.

items.title: UI primitives import full Lucide icon library bundle
items.action: Audit used Lucide icons and measure post-build tree-shaking; inline or pre-select only needed icons if tree-shaking proves ineffective.
items.rationale: Likely already tree-shaken in production builds, so impact is speculative pending measurement.

items.title: seedExistingTabs builds full spaceByGroupByWindow map on every boot without memoization
items.action: Optionally cache the spaceByGroupByWindow map in store.state to avoid rebuilding; lower priority than connector lazy-loading.
items.rationale: O(n*m) work happens once per boot with bounded impact, the weakest perf item.

items.title: SettingsSchema and DEFAULTS use double-unknown casts masking Zod-type mismatch risk
items.action: Replace the DEFAULTS `as unknown as Settings` cast in settings.ts:296 with a `satisfies Record<keyof Settings, ...>` form and comment the buildSchema cast (already guarded by AssertEqual).
items.rationale: Only the unguarded DEFAULTS cast carries real risk (a missing default widens silently); the schema cast is already protected.

items.title: $effect in SmartFolder.svelte mutates $state object keys without bounding growth
items.action: Add a trim step in the SmartFolder.svelte:140 effect removing lastSeenById keys no longer in folderBindings or the current items window.
items.rationale: Mild unbounded memory growth over long sessions with rotating feed items, low severity and slow to manifest.

items.title: Biome noRestrictedImports background/ rule duplicates handlers/ exclusion pattern
items.action: Consolidate the two background/** override entries in biome.json into one using includes exclusions and remove the redundant !**/handlers patterns.
items.rationale: Config-auditability cleanup with no current functional gap, lowest type-safety priority.

items.title: Boundary-divert listener forwards page-controlled URL into openUrl (mitigated)
items.action: No change today; keep openUrl's http(s) re-validation authoritative and add an explicit scheme check at the index.ts:238 enqueue site if openUrl is ever refactored to trust callers.
items.rationale: Already defended by sender.id check and the openUrl sink guard; flagged only as a single bus-bypassing path to keep hardened.

items.title: Connector PATs stored unencrypted in chrome.storage.local (expected for platform)
items.action: Add a test asserting tokens never appear in broadcastState payloads or logs and document local-plaintext storage in the options UI.
items.rationale: Inherent MV3 constraint with a disciplined single-accessor module and no code defect; only hygiene-maintenance remains.

items.title: surface-boot.ts applyDensityToDocument not unit-tested
items.action: Add apps/extension/src/shared/surface-boot.test.ts covering applyDensityToDocument for normal (delete), compact, and comfort modes using jsdom.
items.rationale: Small pure helper with implicit-only branch coverage, low risk.

items.title: platform.ts getModifierLabel Mac vs non-Mac branch not unit-tested
items.location: apps/extension/src/shared/platform.ts — no dedicated test file; getModifierLabel only used via components
items.action: Add apps/extension/src/shared/platform.test.ts mocking navigator.userAgent for Mac/Windows/Edge/Chrome to cover getModifierLabel and getBrowserScheme branches.
items.rationale: UA-branch helper with cosmetic output, lowest-risk testing gap.

items.title: extension-store-publish tasks.md: status header contradicts checked tasks
items.action: Replace the stale 'not yet implemented' header in tasks.md with current status: §1 and §2 implemented, §3 pending maintainer setup.
items.rationale: Stale header is misleading but contradicted by visible checked tasks, low blast radius.

items.title: github-repo-and-ci tasks §2.2: agreed fan-out deviation not in design.md
items.action: Add a design decision (e.g. D6) in github-repo-and-ci/design.md recording the fan-out restructure and noting D3's pnpm -r verify is no longer accurate.
items.rationale: Policy requires agreed deviations reach design.md, but the tasks note already records it so the gap is documentation-completeness only.

items.title: ResultRow.svelte and overlay.css hard-code row-gap: 2px for Comfort-density layout
items.action: Replace row-gap: 2px with row-gap: var(--row-gap) in ResultRow.svelte:140 and overlay.css:312 so the comfort two-line layout is density-aware.
items.rationale: Comfort layout uses the wrong gap literal, a minor density-fidelity slip.

items.title: FaviconTile.svelte drift-in (150ms) and favoriting (320ms) animations bypass motion tokens
items.action: Replace 320ms with var(--motion-slow) in favicon-tile-favoriting and align 150ms drift-in to var(--motion-fast) (or add --motion-subtle).
items.rationale: Motion literals where 320ms exactly matches an existing token, easy token alignment with minor visual stakes.

items.title: SpaceEditor.svelte re-derives accent from raw oklch instead of canonical token formula
items.action: Define light-theme accent tokens in tokens.css under a [data-theme='light'] scope and reference them in SpaceEditor.svelte instead of re-authoring the oklch formula inline.
items.rationale: Light-theme accent literals won't track tokens.css formula changes, a localized drift risk.

items.title: overlay.css .enable-source transition uses hard-coded 120ms ease instead of motion tokens
items.action: Add --motion-fast and --ease-standard to overlay.css's local token block and use them in the .enable-source transition instead of 120ms ease.
items.rationale: Single transition off the motion-token system, very low impact.

items.title: SpaceEditor.svelte field-label uses hard-coded font: 500 11px instead of type-scale tokens
items.action: Replace font: 500 11px/1.2 in SpaceEditor.svelte:539 with font-size: var(--text-xs) and font-weight: var(--weight-medium).
items.rationale: One label rule duplicating type tokens, trivial drift.

items.title: newtab.css stagger delays are hard-coded ms values with no token
items.action: Add scoped --stagger-1..4 custom properties on .home in newtab.css with a comment documenting the rhythm and reference them in the animation-delay rules.
items.rationale: Intentional stagger offsets that are merely undocumented/fragile, not wrong.

items.title: sidebar/app.css uses #000 in mask-image gradient (semantically correct)
items.action: Add an inline comment (/* mask channel: fully opaque */) on app.css:162-163 documenting why #000 is exempt from the token rule.
items.rationale: No functional issue, purely a future-linter/reviewer clarity note — lowest visual priority.

items.title: No architecture integrity violations found
items.action: No code change required; optionally add a doc note in docs/architecture.md mentioning overlay.ts's sanctioned ui/favicon.ts import for consistency.
items.rationale: Clean audit with zero violations across 338 files; only an optional documentation-consistency note remains.
